systheme.__description:
Get the current Windows color theme, as a string value :
  • "dark" : current Windows color theme is dark
  • "light" : current Windows color theme is light
systheme.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("ui.systheme example", 320, 200)
win:center()

local button = ui.Button(win, ui.systheme)
button:center()

function button:onClick()  
    ui.theme = ui.theme == "dark" and "light" or "dark"
    button.text = ui.theme
end

ui.run(win):wait()

opendialog.__returns: The function returns the selected file(s) as File object(s) or nil if no files have been choosen.
opendialog.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- open a dialog to choose an existing file
local file = ui.opendialog()

-- checks if a file has been selected
if file ~= nil then
	ui.info("File path : "..file.fullpath.."\nFile size : "..file.size.." bytes\nFile creation date : "..file.created.."\n")
end
opendialog.__results: File, ... | nil

__params.__description: An optional string that indicates the title of the dialog box (use the default system title if not specified).
An optional boolean value that indicates if the dialog allow multiple files selection or not.

An optional string that indicates filters. A filter is used to display only files with a specified pattern. If omitted, the dialog shows all files.
A filter is composed of a and a field, each separated by the character "|".For example, the string "All files (*.*)|*.*|Text files (*.txt)|*.txt" defines 2 filters :

  • A filter named "All files (*.*)" that correspond to the search pattern "*.*"
  • A filter named "Text files (*.txt)" that correspond to the search pattern "*.txt"

opendialog.__description: Displays a dialog box for the user to choose a file or set of files to be opened. The file(s) must exist physically on the drive.
opendialog.__tinydesc: Show a Windows open dialog box to select one or more files

allowdrop.__description: Get or set the Tree's ability to be used as the target of a drag-and-drop operation
allowdrop.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:onDrop() sample", 440, 380)
local widget = ui.Tree(win, {"Drop it here !"})
widget.allowdrop = true
widget:center()

-- set a onDrop() event
function widget:onDrop(kind, content)
  ui.info("kind: '"..kind.."'\ncontent: "..tostring(content), "Content dropped on Tree")
end

ui.run(win):wait()
allowdrop.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Tree is a drag and drop target

width.__description: Get or set the Tree width. The width starts from 0 (left side of the Tree) and increases to the right direction.
width.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Tree.width sample", 320, 230)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2"}, 100, 10)
local inc_btn = ui.Button(win, "Increase Tree.width", 40, 200)
local dec_btn = ui.Button(win, "Decrease Tree.width", inc_btn.x+inc_btn.width+6, inc_btn.y)

inc_btn.delta = 10
dec_btn.delta = -10

local function onClick(self)
	tree.width = tree.width + self.delta
end

inc_btn.onClick = onClick
dec_btn.onClick = onClick

-- shows the Window
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

show.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:show() sample", 320, 200)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"LuaRT"}, 110, 80)
tree.selected = tree.items[1]

tree.font = "Impact"
tree.fontsize = 18

-- Spawn a Task to hide/show tab every 500ms
async(function()
    while true do
    tree:hide()  
    sleep(500)
    tree:show()
    sleep(500)
  end
end)

ui.run(win):wait()
show.__description: Show and activate the Tree (events can now be fired).

onDrop.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:onDrop() sample", 640, 480)
local widget = ui.Tree(win, {"Drop it here !"})
widget.allowdrop = true
widget:center()

-- set a onDrop() event
function widget:onDrop(kind, content)
	ui.info("kind: "..kind.."\ncontent: "..tostring(content), "Content dropped on Tree")
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string representing the kind of content dropped on the Tree.

The content dropped on the Tree, the type of which depends on the following table:

kindcontent
"text"string
"files"table
(list of File and Directory)
"unkown"nil

onDrop.__description: This event is fired when the user has dropped the mouse over the Tree.
onDrop.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Tree received a drag and drop content

onChange.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Tree:onChange() sample", "fixed", 320, 240)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2", ["Item 3"] = {"Subitem1", "Subitem2"}}, 110, 40)
local edited = nil
win:status("Doubleclick on an item to edit it")

-- Tree onDoubleClick() event
function tree:onDoubleClick(item)
	edited = tree.selected.text
	tree.selected:edit()
end

-- Tree onChangek() event
function tree:onChange(item, action)
	if action == "edited" then
		win:status(edited.." has been renamed to "..item.text)
	end
end

-- show the Window
win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end

__params.__description:
If an item have been removed, this argument represent the item text as a string
If an item have been edited, this argument represent the edited TreeItem.

A string that represent the change that occured :

  • "removed" : TreeItem has been removed.
  • "edited" : TreeItem has been edited.

onChange.__description: This event is fired when an item have been removed or edited by the user after a call to TreeItem:edit().
onChange.__tinydesc: Event fired when items in the Tree have been modified

items.__access: read/write iterable
items.__description: Provides access to the list of Tree items.
items.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Tree.items sample", "fixed", 320, 240)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Iterate over Tree.items", 104, 10)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {}, 110, 40)
tree.style ="icons"

tree.items = {
		"Item 1",
		"Item 2",
		["Item 3"] = {
			"Subitem1",
			"Subitem2"
		}
}

function button:onClick()
	for item in each(tree.items) do
		item:loadicon(sys.env.windir.."\\system32\\user32.dll", 6)
	end
end

-- show the Window
win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end

Tree.__description: The Tree widget displays a hierarchical list of expandable items

font.__description:
Get the Tree font, a string value that represent the font name.
To set the Tree.font property, you can assign a string that represent either a font name of an installed system font, or a font file (*.ttf, *.fon...). A File can also be provided, representing a font file.
Note that only the font family is changed. The font style and font size are not affected (see the Tree.fontstyle and the Tree.fontsize properties).
By default, an Tree uses the same font as its parent widget
font.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree.font sample", 320, 200)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2", "Item 3"}, 106, 20)

tree.font = "Fixedsys"
	
win:show()
			
-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

border.__description:
Get or set the Tree border visibility, a true value means that the border is shown, a false value means that the border is hidden.

Example
border.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Tree.border sample", 320, 260)


-- create a simple centered list 
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item1", "Item2", "Item3"})
tree:center()

local button = ui.Button(win, "Hide/Show border", 90, 10)

-- Hides/Shows Tree border when button is clicked
function button:onClick()
  tree.border = not tree.border
end

ui.run(win):wait()
border.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Tree border is visible

fontstyle.__description:
Get or set the Tree font style, a table value that contains the following keys/values :
  • "italic" : set to true if the font is in italic.
  • "underline" : set to true if the font is underlined.
  • "strike" : set to true if the font is striked.
  • "thin" : set to true if the font is thin.
  • "bold" : set to true if the font is bold.
  • "heavy" : set to true if the font is heavy.
fontstyle.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree.fontstyle sample", 320, 240)
ui.Label(win, "Font size :", 100, 10)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2", "Item 3"}, 106, 40)

-- create a font style tree
local cb = ui.Combobox(win, { tree.fontsize, tree.fontsize+2, tree.fontsize+4, tree.fontsize+6, tree.fontsize+8 }, 150, 6, 80)
cb.selected = cb.items[1] 

-- change tree.fontstyle upon selection
function cb:onSelect(item)
	tree.fontstyle = { [item.text] = true }
	win:status("Tree fontstyle : "..item.text)
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

readonly.__description:
Controls whether the Tree is editable by the user, a true value meaning that the Tree is readonly (the default).

Example
readonly.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("List.border sample", 320, 270)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item1", "Item2", "Item3"})
tree:center()

local button = ui.Button(win, "Enable Tree item edition", 80, 10)

function update_status()
    win:status(tree.readonly and "Tree is not editable by user" or "Tree is editable by user")
end

function button:onClick()
  tree.readonly = not tree.readonly
  button.text = tree.readonly and "Enable Tree item edition" or "Disable Tree item edition"
  update_status()
end

update_status()

ui.run(win):wait()
readonly.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Tree is editable by the user

remove.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree:remove() sample", "fixed", 310, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Select item  :", 10, 30)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, { ["RootItem1"] = {"RootItem1.SubItem1", "RootItem1.SubItem2"}, "RootItem2", "RootItem3"}, label.x+label.width+6, label.y-4)
local removebtn = ui.Button(win, "Remove item", tree.x+tree.width+6, label.y-4)

removebtn.enabled = false

function removebtn:onClick()
	-- removes current selected item
	-- alternative 1: tree.remove(item.selected)
	-- alternative 2: tree.selected:remove()
	tree:remove(tree.selected.text)
end

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
	removebtn.enabled = tree.selected ~= nil
until not win.visible

__params.__description: A string, representing the item's text or the TreeItem to be removed.

remove.__tinydesc: Remove an item from the Tree

toback.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:toback() sample", 400, 300)

local widget = ui.Tree(win, "I'm a Tree")
widget:center()

local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me to put me in the back")
button:center()
button:show()

-- Button:onClick() event
function button:onClick()
	self:toback()
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

__params.__description: The widget below which the Tree will be.

toback.__description: Puts the Tree behind all other widgets, or behind the specified widget.
toback.__tinydesc: Puts the widget to the back

onShow.__example:

-- create a window

local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me !", 125, 30)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2"}, 120, 80)
tree:hide()

	sleep(2000)
	tree:hide()
end

ui.run(win):wait()
onShow.__description: This event is fired when the Tree is shown (with a call to Tree:show() or setting the Tree.visible property to true).
onShow.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Tree is shown

onMouseUp.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:onMouseUp() sample", 400, 300)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Move the mouse over the Tree, then press and release a mouse button!")
label.textalign = "center"
label.align = "top"

tree:center()

end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string indicating the button that has been released : "right", "middle" or "left".
The horizontal position of the mouse in the Tree area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Tree area (zero means the top border of the widget).

onMouseUp.__description: This event is fired when the user releases a mouse button while beeing over the Tree.
onMouseUp.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user releases a mouse button over the Tree

Tree.__tinydesc: Object representation of a hierarchical view of text elements

The horizontal position of the mouse in the Tree area (zero means the left border of the widget).

onMouseDown.__description: This event is fired when the user presses a mouse button while beeing over the Tree.
onMouseDown.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user presses a mouse button over the Tree

clear.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree:clear() sample", "fixed", 410, 220)
local label = ui.Label(win, "New item  :", 10, 25)
local entry =  ui.Entry(win, "", label.x+label.width+6, label.y-4)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Add item", entry.x+entry.width+6, label.y-6)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {}, button.x+button.width+6, label.y-4)
local clearbtn = ui.Button(win, "Clear all", tree.x+tree.width+6, label.y-4)

function clearbtn:onClick()
	tree:clear()
end

function tree:onChange()
	win:status("Tree.count = "..tree.count)
end

function button:onClick()
	-- add new item in the Tree adn select it
	tree.selected = tree:add(entry.text)
  entry.text = ""
end

-- enable button only if entry.text is not empty
function entry:onChange()
	button.enabled = #entry.text > 0
end

entry.onSelect = button.onClick

entry:onChange()
tree:onChange()
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
clear.__description: Remove all items and subitems from the Tree.
clear.__tinydesc: Remove all items from the Tree

onContext.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- creates a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:onContext() event sample", 320, 260)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item1", "Item2", "Item3"}, 110, 40)

-- Tree:onContext event
function tree:onContext(item)
	-- show popup menu if onContext event occured on an item
	if item ~= nil then 
		local menu = ui.Menu("Delete TreeItem")
		menu.items[1].onClick = function (self)
			item:remove()
		end
		win:popup(menu)
	end
end

win:show()

-- update window
while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end

__params.__description: The TreeItem that was right-clicked, or nil if the right click occurred elsewhere than on an item in the Tree.

onContext.__description: This event is fired when a right-click occured on the Tree.
onContext.__tinydesc: Event fired when a right click occured on the Tree

local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"TreeItem 1", "TreeItem 2", "TreeItem 3"}, 100, 10)

-- Tree:onHover event

end

-- Tree:onLeave event

	win:status("The Tree Widget is no longer hovered")
end

win:status("")

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Tree area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Tree area (zero means the top border of the widget).

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the move :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "right": true if right mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "middle": true if middle mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed, false otherwise
  • "shift": true if the SHIFT mouse button is pressed, false otherwise

onHover.__description: This event is fired when the user moves the mouse pointer over the Tree.
onHover.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user moves the mouse pointer over the Tree

add.__returns: This function returns the last added TreeItem
add.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree:add() sample", "fixed", 340, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "New item  :", 10, 30)
local entry =  ui.Entry(win, "", label.x+label.width+6, label.y-4)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Add item", entry.x+entry.width+6, label.y-6)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {}, button.x+button.width+6, label.y-4)

button.enabled = false

function button:onClick()
	-- add new item in the Tree and select it
	tree.selected = tree:add(entry.text)

	button.enabled = #entry.text > 0
end

entry.onSelect = button.onClick
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
add.__results: TreeItem
add.__description: Add one or more root items to the Tree.
add.__tinydesc: Add one or more items to the Tree

style.__description:
Get or set the current style, a string value, that specifies the Tree appearance :
  • "text" : Tree will display only text items (default).
  • "icons" : Tree will display text items with their icons, using TreeItem:loadicon().
When setting the style property from "icons" to "text" all previously loaded icons are preserved.
style.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("tree.style sample", "fixed", 280, 240)
local label = ui.Label(win, "DoubleClick on an application :", 10, 30)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {}, label.x, label.y+20, 180, 160)
local checkicons = ui.Checkbox(win, "Show icons", tree.x+tree.width+12, label.y)

local applications = {
	Notepad = "c:\\Windows\\notepad.exe",
	Console = 'c:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe'
}

-- fill the Tree with items
for app, path in pairs(applications) do 
	tree.selected = tree:add(app)
  tree.selected:loadicon(path)
end

function tree:onDoubleClick(item)
	sys.cmd("start "..applications[item.text])
end

function checkicons:onClick()
	tree.style = self.checked and "icons" or "text"
	win:status("Tree style : "..tree.style:capitalize())
end

checkicons:onClick()
win:show()

repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Tree area.
The vertical position of the mouse in the Tree area.

onClick.__description: This event is fired when the user has clicked on the Tree.
onClick.__tinydesc: Event occurs when the user clicks on the Tree

tofront.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:tofront() sample", 400, 300)

local widget = ui.Tree(win, {"I'am a Tree"})
widget:center()

local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me to bring Tree to front")
button:center()
button:show()

-- Button:onClick() event
function button:onClick()
	widget:tofront()
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

__params.__description: The widget above which the Tree will be.

tofront.__description: Brings the Tree in front of all other widgets, or in front of the specified widget.

__params.__description: An object that will own the Tree. Parent objects can be any of Window, Groupbox, TabItem and Panel
A table containing a list of strings with or without tables values (each string will be inserted in the Tree, tables represent subitems).
An optional number that indicates the Tree horizontal position, in pixels. Zero means the left border of the parent.
An optional number that indicates the Tree vertical position, in pixels. Zero means the top border of the parent.
An optional number that indicates the Tree width in pixels.
An optional number that indicates the Tree height in pixels.

constructor.__description: The Tree constructor returns a Tree value representing a scrollable tree of items.
constructor.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("ui.Tree() constructor sample", "fixed", 320, 200)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2", ["Item 3"] = {"SubItem1", "SubItem2"}}, 106, 20)

-- show the Window
win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end

x.__description: Get or set the Tree horizontal position. The horizontal position start from 0 (left side of the parent widget) and increase to the right (right side of the parent widget).
x.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Tree.x sample", 600, 300)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Change Tree position", 240)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {'Item 1', 'Item 2'}, 10, 140)

function button:onClick()
	tree.x  = math.random(10, win.width-tree.width)
	tree.y  = math.random(40, win.height-tree.height)
end

-- shows the window
win:show()

-- update user interface and increase Tree horizontal position
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

parent.__results:
Window | Groupbox
TabItem | Panel
parent.__description: Get the parent object that owns the Tree.
parent.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Tree.parent sample", "fixed", 320, 240)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Get Tree parent", 116, 10)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Sam", "David", "Jane"}, 104, 40)

function button:onClick()
	ui.info("Tree parent is "..tostring(tree.parent))
end

-- show the Window
win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end

onCreate.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- creates a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:onCreate() event sample", 320, 200)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item1", "Item2", Item3 = {"SubItem1", "SubItem2"}}, 100, 20, 150, 160)

-- Tree:onCreate event to set the font properties
function tree:onCreate()
	tree.font = "Segoe UI"
	tree.style = "icons"
	tree.fontsize = "9"
end

win:show()

-- update Tree.text at each second
while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end
onCreate.__description:
This event is fired when the Tree object has just been created (just after the Tree:constructor() call).
This event is particularly interesting when you want to initialize its properties.
onCreate.__tinydesc: Event fired when Tree has just been created

align.__description:
Get or set the Tree alignment, which position and size will be aligned relative to its parent (alignment persists even if the size of the parent changes).

This property uses a string to specify the Tree alignment :

  • "all" : the Tree will be aligned along all the parent borders (and will cover the entire parent client area).
  • "bottom" : the Tree will be aligned along the bottom border of the parent, preserving its height.
  • "top" : the Tree will be aligned along the the top border of the parent, preserving its height.
  • "right" : the Tree will be aligned along the right border of the parent, preserving its width.
  • "left" : the Tree will be aligned along the left border of the parent, preserving its width.
align.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:align() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Tree
local widget = ui.Tree(win, "I'm a bottom aligned Tree :)", 25, 25)

-- align the Tree to the bottom of its parent 
widget.align = "bottom"

-- shows the Window
win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end
align.__tinydesc: Align the Tree relative to its parent

cursor.__description:
Get or set the image of the mouse cursor as it hovers over the Tree. Mouse cursors are represented by strings :
  • "arrow" : the default mouse cursor.
  • "cross" : a crosshair cursor.
  • "working" : the standard arrow with a small hourglass.
  • "hand" : a hand cursor.
  • "help" : the standard arrow with a small question mark.
  • "ibeam" : the default I-Beam text cursor.
  • "forbidden" : a slashed circle cursor.
  • "cardinal" : a four-pointed arrow pointing up, down, right, and left.
  • "horizontal" : a double-pointed arrow pointing right and left.
  • "vertical" : a double-pointed arrow pointing up and down.
  • "leftdiagonal" : a double-pointed arrow pointing topright down to the left.
  • "rightdiagonal" :a double-pointed arrow pointing topleft down to the right.
  • "up" : an arrow pointing up.
  • "wait" : a hourglass cursor.
  • "none" : no cursor is displayed.
The tree.cursor property affects only the Tree area.
cursor.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree.cursor sample", 320, 200)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Hover Me !", "Item 1", "Item 2"}, 106, 20)
win:show()

-- set the mouse cursor when hovering the tree
tree.cursor = "hand"

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
cursor.__tinydesc: Get/set the image of the mouse cursor as it hovers over the Tree

enabled.__description: Get or set the tree ability to respond to mouse, and any other events. When set to false, disable the Tree (the user will not be able to interact with it), and tree's events won't be fired anymore.
enabled.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree.enabled sample", 320, 200)
ui.Tree(win, {"I'm disabled :(", "Item 1", "Item 2"}, 106, 20).enabled = false

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
enabled.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Tree responds to mouse and other events

height.__description: Get or set the height of the Tree. The height starts from 0 (top side of the Tree) and increase to the bottom direction.
height.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Tree.height sample", 320, 300)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2"}, 100, 10)
local inc_btn = ui.Button(win, "Increase Tree.height", 40, 270)
local dec_btn = ui.Button(win, "Decrease Tree.height", inc_btn.x+inc_btn.width+6, inc_btn.y)

inc_btn.delta = 10
dec_btn.delta = -10

local function onClick(self)
	tree.height = tree.height + self.delta
end

inc_btn.onClick = onClick
dec_btn.onClick = onClick

-- shows the Window
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

sort.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Tree:sort() sample", "fixed", 320, 240)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Sort tree", 130, 10)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Sam", "David", "Jane"}, 104, 40)

	win:status("Tree sorted")

win:status("Tree is not sorted")
-- show the Window

__params.__description: An optional boolean value that specifies to sort all levels of child items below the root items, defaulting to false.

sort.__description: Sort Tree root items by ascending order.

onSelect.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Tree:onSelect() sample", 320, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Name : ", 80, 25)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Sam", "Mary", "John"}, label.x + label.width + 8, label.y - 4, 100)

function tree:onSelect(item)

end

win:show()

while win.visible do

__params.__description: The current TreeItem selected.

onSelect.__description: Event fired when the user has choosen an item in the Tree.
onSelect.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user selects an item in the Tree

visible.__description:
Get or set the Tree visibility on screen, a true value means that the Tree is shown, a false value means that the Tree is hidden.
The tree.visible property is also affected by the Tree:show() and Tree:hide() methods.
visible.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple tree 
local win = ui.Window("Tree.visible sample", 320, 240)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item1", "Item2", "Item3"}, 100, 70)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click to hide tree", 106, 10)

-- hides the tree and shows it again after 2 sec
function button:onClick()
	tree.visible = false
	button.enabled = false
	sleep(2000)
	button.enabled = true
	tree.visible = true
end

ui.run(win):wait()
visible.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Tree appears on screen

count.__description: Get the current root items count in the Tree, subitems not included.
count.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Tree.count sample", "fixed", 360, 220)
local label = ui.Label(win, "New item  :", 30, 30)
local entry =  ui.Entry(win, "", label.x+label.width+6, label.y-4)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Add item", entry.x+entry.width+6, label.y-6)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {}, button.x+button.width+6, label.y-4)

function button:onClick()
	-- add new item in the Tree adn select it
	tree.selected = tree:add(entry.text)
	entry.text = ""
	win:status("Tree.count = "..tree.count)
end

-- enable button only if entry.text is not empty
function entry:onChange()
	button.enabled = #entry.text > 0
end

entry.onSelect = button.onClick

entry:onChange()
win:status("Tree.count = 0")
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
count.__tinydesc: Get the count of items in the Tree

windows.__description: Get a table, representing a list of all created Window objects, even if not visible.
windows.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local x = 0
local y = 0

for i = 1,10 do
    local win = ui.Window("Window #"..i, 320, 200)
    win.x = x
    win.y = y
    x = x+50
    y = y+30
end

async(function()
    for win in each(ui.windows) do
        win:show()
        sleep(500)
        win:hide()
    end        
end)

waitall()
windows.__tinydesc: Get a list of all created Window objects

-- checks if a folder has been selected

	-- Outputs the number of the directory entries

end

__params.__description: An optional string that gives the user indication for the selection.

selectdir.__description: Displays a shell dialog box to choose a folder.
selectdir.__tinydesc: Open a shell dialog box to choose a folder

subitems.__example:

  item.subitems = { item.text..".SubItem1", item.text..".SubItem2" }

expand.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("TreeItem:expand() sample", 320, 220)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {Item1 = {"SubItemA", "SubItemB", "SubItemC"}, "Item2", "Item3"}, 60, 40, 200, 160)

function button:onClick()
  tree.selected:expand()
end

__params.__description:
An optional boolean value indicating if the item should expand (true) or collapse (false).
If omitted, the TreeItem will toggle and collapse if it is already expanded, or expand if it is collapsed.

expand.__description: Expands or collapses the subitems associated with the TreeItem.
expand.__tinydesc: Expand or collapse the subitems of the TreeItem

previous.__results: TreeItem | nil
previous.__description: Retrieves the previous sibling item before the TreeItem
previous.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("TreeItem.previous sample", 320, 220)

local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"TreeItem1", "TreeItem2", "TreeItem3"}, 60, 40, 200, 160)
tree.selected = tree.items["TreeItem1"]
tree.selected.subitems = {"SubItem1", "SubItem2"}

ui.Button(win, "Next", 76, 10).onClick = function (self)
  local next = tree.selected.next
  if next then
    tree.selected = next
  end
end

ui.Button(win, "Previous", 156, 10).onClick = function (self)
  local prev = tree.selected.previous
  if prev then
    tree.selected = prev
  end
end

ui.run(win):wait()

ui.Button(win, "Show Item '51'", 106, 10).onClick = function (self)

end

win:show()

while win.visible do

TreeItem.__description: The TreeItem widget represents an expandable item within a Tree

add.__returns: This function returns the last added TreeItem
add.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("TreeItem:add() sample", 320, 220)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {}, 60, 40, 200, 160)
tree.style = "icons"

local function parse(item, dir)
    if dir ~= nil then
        for entry in each(dir) do
            local newitem = item:add(entry.name)
            newitem:loadicon(entry)
            ui.update()
            if is(entry, sys.Directory) then
                parse(newitem, entry)
            end
        end
    end
end

ui.Button(win, "Choose a directory", 106, 10).onClick = function (self)
    tree:clear()
    parse(tree, ui.dirdialog())
end

win:show()

while win.visible do
    ui.update()
end
add.__results: TreeItem
add.__description: Add one or more subitems to the TreeItem.
add.__tinydesc: Add one or more subitems to the TreeItem

next.__results: TreeItem | nil
next.__description: Retrieves the next sibling item after the TreeItem
next.__example:

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("TreeItem.next sample", 320, 220)

local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"TreeItem1", "TreeItem2", "TreeItem3"}, 60, 40, 200, 160)
tree.selected = tree.items["TreeItem1"]
tree.selected.subitems = {"SubItem1", "SubItem2"}

ui.Button(win, "Next", 76, 10).onClick = function (self)
  local next = tree.selected.next
  if next then
    tree.selected = next
  end
end

ui.Button(win, "Previous", 156, 10).onClick = function (self)
  local prev = tree.selected.previous
  if prev then
    tree.selected = prev
  end
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description:
Represent any of the following possible icon locations :

  • A string which represents the path to an ".ico" icon file, or gets the icon associated with the provided file/directory.
  • A Widget object, whose icon will be used by the item.
  • A Directory or File object, representing an ".ico" file, or gets the icon associated with the provided file/directory.
  • A Buffer object, whose binary content will represent the icon.

When no argument or a nil path value is provided, the TreeItem icon will be removed.
The icon index, an optional number starting from 1, that represent the icon to select.

loadicon.__description: Loads an item icon, displayed to the left of the item's text.
loadicon.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("TreeItem:loadicon() sample", 316, 246)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Disk", "Network", "Search"}, 10, 40)
local icons = { Disk = 8, Network = 19, Search = 23 }

-- set Tree style property to "icons" to view TreeItems icons
tree.style = "icons"

-- all icons are in a resources directory
for item, icon in pairs(icons) do
    tree.items[item]:loadicon("shell32.dll", icon)
end

-- shows the Window
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
    ui.update()
until not win.visible

edit.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Tree:onChange() sample", "fixed", 320, 240)
local tree = ui.Tree(win, {"Item 1", "Item 2", ["Item 3"] = {"Subitem1", "Subitem2"}}, 110, 40)
local edited = nil
win:status("Doubleclick on an item to edit it")

-- Tree onDoubleClick() event
function tree:onDoubleClick(item)
  edited = tree.selected.text
  tree.selected:edit()
end

-- Tree onChange() event
function tree:onChange(item, action)
  if action == "edited" then
    win:status(edited.." has been renamed to "..item.text)
  end
end

-- show the Window
win:show()

while win.visible do
  ui.update()
end
edit.__description:
Begins in-place editing of the specified TreeItem's caption.
When the user has done editing it, the parent Tree will receive a Tree:onChange() event.
edit.__tinydesc: Begin in-place editing of the TreeItem text

kindcontent

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the window's client area.
The vertical position of the mouse in the window's client area.

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the move :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "right": true if right mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "middle": true if middle mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed, false otherwise

local ui = require "ui"

__params.__description:
A string that represent the key name.

Key names are defined by the following rules :
  • For digit keys 0..9, the name is the digit as a string : "0", "1" ...
  • For alphabetical keys A..Z, the name is the character as a string : "A", "B" ...
  • Any other key is represented by the Windows virtual key as a string : "VK_SPACE", "VK_RETURN", "VK_SHIFT"...
  • The full list of Windows virtual keys is available here

onKey.__description:
This event is fired when a key was pressed.

Parameters
onKey.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user press a key

onTrayHover.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window that will stay hidden
local win = ui.Window("Window.onTrayHover() sample", 320, 200)

-- loads a notification tray icon
win:loadtrayicon(sys.env.WINDIR.."/system32/shell32.dll", 44)

local canexit = false
local tooltips = { "The stars are brilliant this evening", "The sky is clear and stars bright.", "Moon and stars are bright overhead" }

-- Display a random tooltip when mouse hovers the notification icon
function win:onTrayHover()
  self.traytooltip = tooltips[math.floor(math.random(1, 3))]
end

-- Click on the notification icon to exit program
function win:onTrayClick()
  canexit = true
end

-- update user interface
repeat
  ui.update()
until canexit
onTrayHover.__description: This event is fired when the mouse hovers the notification icon of the window, in the tray section of the taskbar
onTrayHover.__tinydesc: Event fired when the mouse hovers the notification icon of the Window

onMouseDown.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Window:onMouseDown() sample", 400, 300)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Move the mouse over the Window and press a mouse button!")
label:center()

-- set a onMouseDown() event : display a status bar with the pressed mouse button and its position
function win:onMouseDown(b, x, y)
  win:status("Mouse button : "..b, "Mouse position : "..x..", "..y)
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string indicating the button that has been pressed : "right", "middle" or "left".
The horizontal position of the mouse in the Window area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Window area (zero means the top border of the widget).

onMouseDown.__description: This event is fired when the user presses a mouse button while beeing over the Window.
onMouseDown.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user presses a mouse button over the Window

onTrayClick.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Window.onTrayClick() sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me to quit !")

button:center()
win:status("Close the Window to show the notify icon")

local canexit = false

-- event fired when notification icon is clicked
function win:onTrayClick()
  -- removes the notification icon
  self:loadtrayicon()
  -- shows the window
  win:show()
end

-- event fired when the window close button is clicked
function win:onClose()
  -- loads and display a notification icon in the tray
  win:loadtrayicon(sys.env.WINDIR.."/system32/shell32.dll", 28)
  -- then hides the window
  self:hide()
  -- and set the notification icon tooltip
  win.traytooltip = "click me to show the window again"
  -- dont close the window after this event
  return false
end

win:show()

-- button:onClick() event (to quit)
function button:onClick()
  canexit = true
end

-- update user interface
repeat
  ui.update()
until canexit
onTrayClick.__description: This event is fired when the user has clicked on the notification icon of the window, in the tray section of the taskbar
onTrayClick.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user clicks on the notification icon of the Window

onCreate.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a personalized template Window Object
local MyWindow = Object(ui.Window)

-- define a custom constructor for MyWindow
function MyWindow:constructor(title)
	ui.Window.constructor(self, title or "MyWindow template", "fixed", 640, 480)
end

-- defines the MyWindow widgets, menus and status bar
function MyWindow:onCreate()
	self:status("Welcome on MyWindow !")
	self.menu = ui.Menu("&File", "&Edit", "&About")
	self.editor = ui.Edit(self, "")
	self.editor.align = "all"
end

-- Align the self.editor widget when resizing MyWindow
function MyWindow:onResize()
	self.editor.align = "all"
end

-- creates two templated MyWindow 
local win = MyWindow("Template 1")
local win2 = MyWindow("Template 2")

win:show()
win2:show()

while win.visible or win2.visible do
	ui.update()
end
onCreate.__description:
This event is fired when the Window object has just been created, just after the Window:constructor() call.
This event is particularly interesting when you want to initialize its properties.
onCreate.__tinydesc: Event fired when Window has just been created

__description: The ui module provides tools for creating graphical user interfaces

allowdrop.__description: Get or set the Entry's ability to be used as the target of a drag-and-drop operation
allowdrop.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Entry:onDrop() sample", 440, 380)
local widget = ui.Entry(win, "Drop it here !")
widget.allowdrop = true
widget:center()

-- set a onDrop() event
function widget:onDrop(kind, content)
  ui.info("kind: '"..kind.."'\ncontent: "..tostring(content), "Content dropped on Entry")
end

ui.run(win):wait()
allowdrop.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Entry is a drag and drop target

width.__description: Get or set the Entry width. The width starts from 0 (left side of the Entry) and increases to the right direction.
width.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Entry.width sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, ">>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>", 50)
local inc_btn = ui.Button(win, "Increase Entry.width !", 15, 50)
local dec_btn = ui.Button(win, "Decrease Entry.width", inc_btn.x+150, inc_btn.y)

local function onClick(self)
	local delta
	if self == inc_btn then
		delta = 10
	else
		delta = -10
	end
	entry.width = entry.width + delta
end

inc_btn.onClick = onClick
dec_btn.onClick = onClick

-- shows the Window
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

show.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Entry:show() sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "LuaRT", 130, 80)

entry.font = "Impact"
entry.fontsize = 18

-- Spawn a Task to hide/show Entry every 500ms
async(function()
    while true do
      entry:show()    
      sleep(500)
      entry:hide()
      sleep(500)
    end
end)

ui.run(win):wait()
show.__description: Show and activate the Entry (events can now be fired).

onDrop.__example:

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Entry:onDrop() sample", 640, 480)
local widget = ui.Entry(win, "Drop it here !")
widget.allowdrop = true
widget:center()

-- set a onDrop() event
function widget:onDrop(kind, content)
	ui.info("kind: "..kind.."\ncontent: "..tostring(content), "Content dropped on Entry")
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string representing the kind of content dropped on the Entry.

The content dropped on the Entry, the type of which depends on the following table:

kindcontent
"text"string
"files"table
(list of File and Directory)
"unkown"nil

onDrop.__description: This event is fired when the user has dropped the mouse over the Entry.
onDrop.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Entry received a drag and drop content

text.__description:
Get or set the Entry text content as a string.
After a change in the Entry.text property, the Entry object do not autosize.
text.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Entry.text sample", 320, 200)

-- add a Entry...
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "LuaRT", 124, 80)

-- ...and a Button 
ui.Button(win, "Show Entry.text", 105, 110).onClick = function (self)
	ui.info("Entry.text = "..entry.text)
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

onChange.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry:onChange() sample", 320, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Enter a number : ", 60, 80)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "", label.x+label.width+10, label.y-4)

function entry:onChange()
	if #self.text > 0 and tonumber(self.text) == nil then
		self.text = self.text:gsub("%D", "")
	end
end

win:status("Enter only numeric characters")
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
	
until not win.visible
onChange.__description: This event is fired when the user has modified the Entry content.
onChange.__tinydesc: Event fired when Entry has just been modified

Window | Groupbox
TabItem | Panel

Entry.__description: The Entry widget provides single-line text input for user data entry

font.__description:
Get the Entry font, a string value that represent the font name.

To set the Entry.font property, you can assign a string that represent either a font name of an installed system font, or a font file (*.ttf, *.fon...).
A File can also be provided, representing a font file.

Note that only the font family is changed. The font style and font size are not affected (see the Entry.fontstyle and the Entry.fontsize properties).
font.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.font sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "", 30,90)
				
entry.fontsize = 12
entry.width = 260
	
entry.text = "Hello World in '"..entry.font.."' "..entry.fontsize.."px"
win:show()
			
-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

textlimit.__description:
Get or set a number value, indicating the maximum length of the text entered.
Setting the property with avalue of 0, permits a maximum of 64000 characters.
textlimit.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.textlimit sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "0", 135, 60, 70)

entry.textlimit = 2
win:status("Enter a number between 0 and 99")

entry.font = "Times New Roman"
entry.fontsize = 24
win:show()

function entry:onSelect()
	if tonumber(entry.text) ~= nil then
		ui.msg("You have entered number "..entry.text)
	else
		ui.error(entry.text.." is not a valid number")
	end
end

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
textlimit.__tinydesc: Get/set the maximum text length of the Entry

fontstyle.__description:
Get or set the Entry font style, a table value that contains the following keys/values :
  • "italic" : set to true if the font is in italic.
  • "underline" : set to true if the font is underlined.
  • "strike" : set to true if the font is striked.
  • "thin" : set to true if the font is thin.
  • "bold" : set to true if the font is bold.
  • "heavy" : set to true if the font is heavy.
Any change in the Entry.font, Entry.fontstyle and Entry.fontsize properties results in an automatic resizing of the Entry so that the content is displayed correctly
fontstyle.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.fontstyle sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "LuaRT", 100, 100)

-- create a combobox
local cb = ui.Combobox(win, { "bold", "italic", "underline", "heavy", "thin" }, 104, 30)
cb.selected = cb.items[5] 

-- change entry.fontstyle upon selection
function cb:onSelect(item)
	entry.fontstyle = { [item.text] = true }
end

entry.font = "Times New Roman"
entry.fontsize = 24
win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

undo.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a fixed Window to hold the buttons
local win = ui.Window("Entry:paste() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Enter text : ", 25, 80)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "", label.x + label.width + 8, label.y-4, 150)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Undo", entry.x + entry.width + 8, entry.y-4)

function button:onClick()
	entry:undo()
end

win:show()
-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
	-- enabled button only if entry.canundo is true
	button.enabled = entry.canundo and true or false
until not win.visible

textalign.__description:
Get or set the Entry text alignment, a string value that represent how the text will be displayed.
  • "left" : the text is aligned to the left edge of the Entry (the default).
  • "right" : the text is aligned to the right edge of the Entry.
  • "center" : the text is aligned in the center of the Entry area.
textalign.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Button.textalign sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "LuaRT", 0, 0, 100)
entry:center()

-- create a combobox
local cb = ui.Combobox(win, { "left", "center", "right" }, 100, 30)
cb:center()
cb.y = cb.y + 32
cb.selected = cb.items[entry.textalign]

-- change label.textalign property upon selection
function cb:onSelect(item)
  entry.textalign = item.text
end

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
ui.run(win):wait()

tooltip.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.tooltip sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "Hover me !", 100, 80)

entry.font = "Corbel"
entry.fontsize = 18

-- set the Entry tooltip
entry.tooltip = "I'm a tooltip"

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

modified.__description: Get or set a boolean value, indicating wether the Entry has been modified.
modified.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.modified sample", 320, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Enter your name : ", 60, 80)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "LuaRT", label.x+label.width+10, label.y-4)

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
	win:status("Entry.modified = "..tostring(entry.modified))
until not win.visible
modified.__tinydesc: Get/set wether Entry's content has been modified

Entry.__tinydesc: Object representation of a single line input control

onMouseDown.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Edit:onMouseDown() sample", 420, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "Move the mouse over this text and press a mouse button!")
entry:center()

-- set a onMouseDown() event : display a status bar with the pressed mouse button and its position
function entry:onMouseDown(b, x, y)
  win:status("Mouse button : "..b, "Mouse position : "..x..", "..y)
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string indicating the button that has been pressed : "right", "middle" or "left".
The horizontal position of the mouse in the Entry area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Entry area (zero means the top border of the widget).

onMouseDown.__description: This event is fired when the user presses a mouse button while beeing over the Entry.
onMouseDown.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user presses a mouse button over the Entry

onHover.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Window:onHover() sample", 320, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Name : ", 80, 75)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "", label.x + label.width + 8, label.y - 4, 100)

function entry:onHover()
	win:status("Field name: Enter a valid name")
end

function entry:onLeave()
	win:status("")
end

win:status()
ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Entry area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Entry area (zero means the top border of the widget).

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the move :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "right": true if right mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "middle": true if middle mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed, false otherwise
  • "shift": true if the SHIFT mouse button is pressed, false otherwise

onHover.__description: This event is fired when the user moves the mouse pointer over the Entry.
onHover.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user moves the mouse pointer over the Entry

onSelect.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Window:onSelect() sample", 320, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Name : ", 80, 75)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "", label.x + label.width + 8, label.y - 4, 100)

entry.tooltip = "Enter your name then press ENTER"

function entry:onSelect()

end

win:show()

end
onSelect.__description: Event fired when the user has pressed the ENTER/RETURN key or when the widget has lost focus.
onSelect.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user has entered a new text

onCreate.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- creates a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Entry:onCreate() event sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "", 150, 70)

-- Entry:onCreate event to set the font properties
function entry:onCreate()
	entry.text = "0"
	entry.font = "Impact"
	entry.fontsize = "24"
	entry.fontstyle = { bold = true }
end

-- Spawn a Task to update Combobox.text every second
async(function()
    while true do
      sleep(1000)
      entry.text = entry.text + 1
    end
end)

ui.run(win):wait()
onCreate.__description:
This event is fired when the Entry object has just been created (just after the Entry:constructor() call).
This event is particularly interesting when you want to initialize its properties.
onCreate.__tinydesc: Event fired when Entry has just been created

height.__description: Get or set the height of the Entry. The height starts from 0 (top side of the Entry) and increase to the bottom direction.
height.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Entry.height sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "Text field", 110, 60)
local inc_btn = ui.Button(win, "Increase Entry.height !", 15, 10)
local dec_btn = ui.Button(win, "Decrease Entry.height", inc_btn.x+150, inc_btn.y)

entry.fontstyle = { bold = true }

function inc_btn:onClick()
	local delta
	if self == inc_btn then
		delta = 10
	else
		delta = -10
	end
	entry.height = entry.height + delta
end

dec_btn.onClick = inc_btn.onClick

-- shows the Window
win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

onHide.__returns: The event returns no value.
onHide.__description: This event is fired when the Entry is hidden (with a call to Entry:hide() or setting the Entry.visible property to false).
onHide.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Entry is hidden

local button = ui.Button(win, "Copy text", entry.x + entry.width + 8, entry.y-4)

function button:onClick()

win:show()

repeat

until not win.visible
copy.__description: Copies the current Entry text to the clipboard.
copy.__tinydesc: Copies the text to the clipboard

fontsize.__description:
Get or set the Entry font size, a number that represent the font size in pixels.
Any change in the Entry.font, Entry.fontstyle and Entry.fontsize properties results in an automatic resizing of the Entry so that the content is displayed correctly

entry.fontsize = 16
entry.text = entry.font.." font, "..entry.fontsize.."px"

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

y.__description: Get or set the Entry vertical position. The vertical position start from 0 (top side of the parent widget) and increase to the bottom side of the parent widget.
y.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Entry.y sample", 300, 600)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "Hello LuaRT !")

-- shows the window

-- update user interface and increase Entry horizontal position
repeat
	ui.update()
	entry.y = entry.y + 1
until not win.visible or (entry.y > 600)

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Entry area.
The vertical position of the mouse in the Entry area.

onClick.__description: This event is fired when the user has clicked on the Entry.
onClick.__tinydesc: Event occurs when the user clicks on the Entry

until not win.visible

canundo.__description: Get a boolean value, indicating wether the user can undo the last Entry operation.
canundo.__example:

-- create a fixed Window to hold the buttons

local label = ui.Label(win, "Enter text : ", 60, 80)

align.__description:
Get or set the Entry alignment, which position and size will be aligned relative to its parent (alignment persists even if the size of the parent changes).

This property uses a string to specify the Entry alignment :

  • "all" : the Entry will be aligned along all the parent borders (and will cover the entire parent client area).
  • "bottom" : the Entry will be aligned along the bottom border of the parent, preserving its height.
  • "top" : the Entry will be aligned along the the top border of the parent, preserving its height.
  • "right" : the Entry will be aligned along the right border of the parent, preserving its width.
  • "left" : the Entry will be aligned along the left border of the parent, preserving its width.
align.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Entry:align() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Entry
local widget = ui.Entry(win, "I'm a bottom aligned Entry :)", 25, 25)

-- align the Entry to the bottom of its parent 
widget.align = "bottom"

-- shows the Window
win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end
align.__tinydesc: Align the Entry relative to its parent

cursor.__description:
Get or set the image of the mouse cursor as it hovers over the Entry. Mouse cursors are represented by strings :
  • "arrow" : the default mouse cursor.
  • "cross" : a crosshair cursor.
  • "working" : the standard arrow with a small hourglass.
  • "hand" : a hand cursor.
  • "help" : the standard arrow with a small question mark.
  • "ibeam" : the default I-Beam text cursor.
  • "forbidden" : a slashed circle cursor.
  • "cardinal" : a four-pointed arrow pointing up, down, right, and left.
  • "horizontal" : a double-pointed arrow pointing right and left.
  • "vertical" : a double-pointed arrow pointing up and down.
  • "leftdiagonal" : a double-pointed arrow pointing topright down to the left.
  • "rightdiagonal" :a double-pointed arrow pointing topleft down to the right.
  • "up" : an arrow pointing up.
  • "wait" : a hourglass cursor.
  • "none" or nil : no cursor is displayed.
The entry.cursor property affects only the Entry area.
cursor.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.cursor sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "Hover Me !", 115, 80)
win:show()

-- set the mouse cursor when hovering Entry
entry.cursor = "hand"

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
cursor.__tinydesc: Get/set the image of the mouse cursor as it hovers over the Entry

enabled.__description: Get or set the Entry ability to respond to mouse, and any other events. When set to false, disable the Entry (the user will not be able to interact with it), and entry's events won't be fired anymore.
enabled.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.enabled sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "I'm disabled :(", 100, 80)

-- disable the Entry
entry.enabled = false
win:show()

-- entry:onClick() event (won't be fired !)
function entry:onClick()
	ui.info("You have clicked on the Entry !!")
end

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
enabled.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Entry responds to mouse and other events

__params.__description: An object that will own the Entry. Parent objects can be any of Window, Groupbox, TabItem and Panel
A string representing the Entry's text.
An optional number that indicates the Entry horizontal position, in pixels. Zero means the left border of the parent.
An optional number that indicates the Entry vertical position, in pixels. Zero means the top border of the parent.
An optional number that indicates the Entry width in pixels, autosized to fit text content if omitted.
An optional number that indicates the Entry height in pixels, autosized to fit text content if omitted.

	ui.update()
until not win.visible

-- create a window

local label = ui.Label(win, "Name : ", 80, 75)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "", label.x + label.width + 8, label.y - 4, 100)

function entry:onHover()

function entry:onLeave()

win:status()

ui.run(win):wait()
onLeave.__description: This event is fired when the mouse cursor leaves the Entry.
onLeave.__tinydesc: Event fired when the mouse cursor leaves the Entry

masked.__description: Get or set a boolean value, indicating wether the user input is visible or hidden by "*" characters.
masked.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.masked sample", 320, 200)
local label = ui.Label(win, "Secret code : ", 60, 80)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "0101111010101", label.x+label.width+10, label.y-4)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Show secret code", 110, 120)

function button:onClick()
	entry.masked = not entry.masked
	button.text = entry.masked and "Show secret code" or "Hide secret code"
end 

-- mask the Entry
entry.masked = true
win:show()

-- entry:onClick() event (won't be fired !)
function entry:onClick()
	ui.info("You have clicked on the Entry !!")
end

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
masked.__tinydesc: Get/set wether user input is visible or masked

visible.__description:
Get or set the Entry visibility on screen, a true value means that the Entry is shown, a false value means that the Entry is hidden.
The entry.visible property is also affected by the Entry:show() and Entry:hide() methods.
visible.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple entry 
local win = ui.Window("Entry.visible sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "LuaRT", 120, 70)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click to hide entry", 110, 10)

entry.font = "Impact"
entry.fontsize = 24

-- hides the Entry and shows it again after 2 sec
function button:onClick()
	entry.visible = false
	button.enabled = false
	sleep(2000)
	button.enabled = true
	entry.visible = true
end

ui.run(win):wait()
visible.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Entry appears on screen

hide.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Entry:hide() sample", 320, 200)
local entry = ui.Entry(win, "LuaRT", 110, 80)

entry.font = "Impact"
entry.fontsize = 18

-- Spawn a Task to hide/show calendar every 500ms
async(function()
    while true do
      entry:hide()  
      sleep(500)
      entry:show()
      sleep(500)
    end
end)

ui.run(win):wait()
hide.__description: Hide and deactivate the Entry (events can no longer be fired).

show.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Button:show() sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click Me (if you can !)", 100, 80)

-- Hides the button when hovering it
function button:onHover()
	self:hide()
end

-- Shows the button when hovering the button area
function win:onHover(x,y buttons)
	if (x > button.x and x < button.x + button.width) or (y > button.y and y < button.y + button.height) then
		button:show()
	end
end

ui.run(win):wait()
show.__description: Show and activate the Button (events can now be fired).

kindcontent

__params.__description:
Represent any of the following possible icon locations :

  • A string which represents the path to an ".ico" icon file, or gets the icon associated with the provided file/directory.
  • A Widget object, whose icon will be used by the Button.
  • A Directory or File object, representing an ".ico" file, or gets the icon associated with the provided file/directory.
  • A Buffer object, whose binary content will represent the icon.
When no argument or a nil path value is provided, the icon will be removed.
The icon index, an optional number starting from 1, that represent the icon to select.

loadicon.__description: Loads a Button icon, displayed to the left of the Button caption.

fontstyle.__description:
Get or set the Button font style, a table value that contains the following keys/values :
  • "italic" : set to true if the font is in italic.
  • "underline" : set to true if the font is underlined.
  • "strike" : set to true if the font is striked.
  • "thin" : set to true if the font is thin.
  • "bold" : set to true if the font is bold.
  • "heavy" : set to true if the font is heavy.
Any change in the Button.font, Button.fontstyle and Button.fontsize properties results in an automatic resizing of the Button so that the content is displayed correctly
fontstyle.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Button.fontstyle sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "LuaRT", 100, 90)

-- create a combobox
local cb = ui.Combobox(win, { "bold", "italic", "underline", "heavy", "thin" }, 94, 30)

-- change button.fontstyle upon selection
function cb:onSelect(item)
	button.fontstyle = { [item.text] = true }
end

button.font = "Times New Roman"
button.fontsize = 24
win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

toback.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Button:toback() sample", 400, 300)

local widget = ui.Button(win, "I'm a Button")
widget:center()

local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me to put me in the back")
button:center()
button:show()

-- Button:onClick() event
function button:onClick()
	self:toback()
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

__params.__description: The widget below which the Button will be.

toback.__description: Puts the Button behind all other widgets, or behind the specified widget.
toback.__tinydesc: Puts the widget to the back

textalign.__description:
Get or set the Button text alignment, a string value that represent how the text will be displayed.
  • "left" : the text is aligned to the left edge of the Button.
  • "right" : the text is aligned to the right edge of the Button.
  • "center" : the text is aligned in the center of the Button area (the default).
textalign.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Button.textalign sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "LuaRT", 0, 0, 100)
button:center()

-- create a combobox
local cb = ui.Combobox(win, { "left", "center", "right" }, 100, 30)
cb:center()
cb.y = cb.y + 32
cb.selected = cb.items[button.textalign]

-- change label.textalign property upon selection
function cb:onSelect(item)
  button.textalign = item.text
end

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
ui.run(win):wait()

onShow.__returns: The event returns no value.
onShow.__description: This event is fired when the Button is shown (with a call to Button:show() or setting the Button.visible property to true).
onShow.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Button is shown

onMouseUp.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Button:onMouseUp() sample", 400, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Move the mouse over this text and press, then release a mouse button!")
button:center()

-- set a onMouseUp() event : display a status bar with the pressed mouse button and its position
function button:onMouseUp(b, x, y)
  win:status("Mouse button : "..b, "Mouse position : "..x..", "..y)
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string indicating the button that has been released : "right", "middle" or "left".
The horizontal position of the mouse in the Button area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Button area (zero means the top border of the widget).

onMouseUp.__description: This event is fired when the user releases a mouse button while beeing over the Button.
onMouseUp.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user releases a mouse button over the Button

center.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Button:center() sample", 320, 200)
ui.Button(win, "Center Me !").onClick = function (self)
                                            self:center()
                                        end
win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
    ui.update()
until not win.visible
center.__description: Centers the Button on the parent widget.
center.__tinydesc: Centers the Button on the parent widget

tooltip.__description:
Get or set the tooltip text that appears when the user moves the mouse over the control, represented as a string value.
Set the property to an empty string to remove the tooltip message.
tooltip.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple window 
local win = ui.Window("Button.tooltip sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Hover me !", 100, 80)

button.font = "Corbel"
button.fontsize = 18

-- set the Button tooltip
button.tooltip = "Click to exit"

function button:onClick()
	win:hide()
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

hastext.__description:
Get or set the button's text visibility on screen, a true value means that the text is shown, a false value means that the text is hidden.
When setting the value to false, the button's text value is lost.
hastext.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple button 
local win = ui.Window("Button.hastext sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me !")
button:loadicon(sys.env.WINDIR.."/system32/shell32.dll", 44)
button:center()

-- hides the button when mouse cursor hovers it
function button:onClick()
  self.hastext = not self.hastext
  if self.hastext then
    self.text = "Click me !"
  end
  button:center()
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the button
repeat
  ui.update()
until not win.visible

onHover.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Button:onHover() sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Move the mouse over this text!", 80, 75)

-- set a onClick() event : display a status bar with the mouse position when the user hovers the button
function button:onHover(x,y buttons)
 win:status("Mouse is inside Button at "..x..", "..y, buttons.left and "LEFT button" or "", buttons.right and "RIGHT button" or "", buttons.middle and "MIDDLE button" or "")
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Button area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Button area (zero means the top border of the widget).

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the move :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "right": true if right mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "middle": true if middle mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed, false otherwise
  • "shift": true if the SHIFT mouse button is pressed, false otherwise

onHover.__description: This event is fired when the user moves the mouse pointer over the Button.
onHover.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user moves the mouse pointer over the Button

onMouseDown.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Button:onMouseDown() sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Move the mouse over this text and press a button!")
button:center()

-- set a onMouseDown() event : display a status bar with the pressed mouse button and its position
function button:onMouseDown(b, x, y)
  win:status("Mouse button : "..b, "Mouse position : "..x..", "..y)
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string indicating the button that has been pressed : "right", "middle" or "left".
The horizontal position of the mouse in the Button area (zero means the left border of the widget).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Button area (zero means the top border of the widget).

onMouseDown.__description: This event is fired when the user presses a mouse button while beeing over the Button.
onMouseDown.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user presses a mouse button over the Button

onHide.__returns: The event returns no value.
onHide.__description: This event is fired when the Button is hidden (with a call to Button:hide() or setting the Button.visible property to false).
onHide.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Button is hidden

fontsize.__description:
Get or set the Button font size, a number that represent the font size in pixels.
Any change in the Button.font, Button.fontstyle and Button.fontsize properties results in an automatic resizing of the Button so that the content is displayed correctly
fontsize.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple button 
local win = ui.Window("Button.fontsize sample", 640, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "", 200, 70)
button.fontsize = 16


function button:onClick()
	self.fontsize = math.random(8, 24)
	self.text = self.font.." font, "..self.fontsize.."px"
end

button:onClick()
win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

y.__description: Get or set the Button vertical position. The vertical position start from 0 (top side of the parent widget) and increase to the bottom side of the parent widget.
y.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Button.y sample", 300, 600)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click Me !")

function button:onClick()
	ui.msg("You caught me !")
end

-- shows the window
win:show()

-- update user interface and increase Button horizontal position
repeat
	ui.update()
	button.y = button.y + 1
until not win.visible or (button.y > 600)

autosize.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Button:autosize() sample", 320, 200)

-- add a tiny Button...
local button = ui.Button(win, "LuaRT", 120, 70)
-- ...with a big font
button.font = "Impact"
button.fontsize = 18
-- resize to a small button
button.width = 20
button.height = 16

-- ...and a Button 
ui.Button(win, "Autosize Button", 112, 10).onClick = function (self)
	button:autosize()
	self.enabled = false
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible
autosize.__description: Resize the Button automatically to accommodate its content.

onContext.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Button:onContext() sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Right-click on this Button !", 80, 75)

-- set a onContext() event : change the Button text

	self.text = "Good job !"
end

win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end
onContext.__description: This event is fired when the user has clicked on the Button with the right mouse button.
onContext.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user right click on the Button

onClick.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a window
local win = ui.Window("Button:onClick() sample", 320, 200)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me !", 100, 75)

-- set a onClick() event : change the button position

	button.x = math.random(win.width-self.width)
	button.y = math.random(win.height-self.height)
end

win:show()

while win.visible do
	ui.update()
end
onClick.__description: This event is fired when the user has clicked on the Button.
onClick.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user left clicks on the Button

x.__description: Get or set the Button horizontal position. The horizontal position start from 0 (left side of the parent widget) and increase to the right (right side of the parent widget).
x.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Button.x sample", 600, 300)
local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me !", 10, 140)

-- set an button.onClick event handler

	self.x = math.random(500)
	self.y = math.random(260)
end

-- shows the window

-- update user interface and increase Button horizontal position
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

tofront.__example:
local ui = require "ui"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Button:tofront() sample", 400, 300)

local widget = ui.Button(win, "I'am a Button")
widget:center()

local button = ui.Button(win, "Click me to bring Button to front")
button:center()
button:show()

-- Button:onClick() event
function button:onClick()
	widget:tofront()
end

win:show()

-- update the user interface until the user closes the Window
